title: prangos bucharica
intro: Prangos bucharica, a perennial plant native to Central Asia, is a member of the carrot family and is also known as Ferula bucharica or Ferula prangos. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its roots and rhizomes, which contain compounds like ferulic acid and ascorbic acid, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Prangos bucharica has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, thanks to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant's roots and rhizomes are often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine practices.
